_status: frozen before evaluation; the evaluation binary embeds this file and records its SHA-256 in every run's metadata. Changing it after results exist is a preregistration deviation and must be reported as such.

regression._decimation_rationale: consecutive cycles of one engine are near-duplicate rows; a fixed stride-20 subsample keeps the pooled training set around 1000 rows so the robust iterative fits stay tractable at 100-engine leave-one-out scale. Fixed a priori.

srcc._channel_rationale: the 14 FD001 sensors that are not constant (s2,s3,s4,s7,s8,s9,s11,s12,s13,s14,s15,s17,s20,s21) in feature-space indices; settings and constant sensors excluded a priori, not by peeking at outcomes
srcc._decimation_rationale: engine trajectories hold 128-362 cycles; consecutive cycles give near-duplicate sources, so a fixed stride-20 subsample keeps per-view source sets around 7-18 points, which is where certified branch-and-bound and leave-one-out refitting stay tractable. The stride is fixed a priori, not tuned on outcomes.
srcc._raw_radii_rationale: raw distances are dominated by the large-magnitude channels (s9 ~ 9050, s14 ~ 8130 with O(10) spread), so the grid brackets that spread analytically
srcc._scaled_radii_rationale: after robust-diagonal scaling each channel contributes O(1); sqrt(14) ~ 3.7 sets the bracket

secom._selection_rule: grid methods are selected on the VALIDATION split only (AUROC for score producers, balanced accuracy for DBSCAN); the selected configuration is then frozen and reported on test; all candidate validation values are published

cmapss_fd003._role: the replication partner of FD001: same turbofan domain and column layout, two fault modes (HPC + fan) instead of one, so a result holding on both FD001 and FD003 is a replication in the preregistered sense

srcc._channel_rationale: the same 14 channels used for FD001; all vary in FD003 too, so the replication pair shares an identical view construction rather than a per-subset re-choice

stream._monitored_rationale: sensor channel 11 (feature index): a degradation-sensitive turbofan sensor; the stream monitors one engine's trajectory of this channel in cycle order
